mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 45% when the trigger activates, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient economic output that offsets higher borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 50% during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency falls below 52% following payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level exceeding 215.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (Kruskal-Wallis p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals weak consumer demand despite aggregate growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates premature economic contraction that triggers immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency drops below 45% during recovery phases, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample validation.
